title: leucanthemum coronopifolium subvar. corsicum
intro: Leucanthemum coronopifolium subvar. corsicum, also known as the Pyrenean or Corsican oxeye daisy, is a subvariety of the Leucanthemum coronopifolium plant, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Leucanthemum coronopifolium subvar. corsicum include relieving symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and menstrual cramps, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
